$comment: Windy Chat ops surface — Class C, multi-service constellation: ~11 services (onboarding, directory, push-gateway, backup, social, translation, agent-roster, media, call-history, hub) behind nginx path-routing at chat.windychat.ai, over a Synapse (Matrix) homeserver. Rev 2026-07-13: the FLEET-HEALTH AGGREGATOR is now real — windy-chat #143 shipped GET /api/v1/ops/health (auth: EPT or account JWT; whitelist-scrubbed; fans out to every service's internal /health + /version plus Synapse). It satisfies get_health + get_status + get_capabilities at once, per the multi-service pattern (windy-contracts docs/MULTI-SERVICE-OPS.md) that this retrofit originated. DEPLOY STATUS: merged to main; LIVE only after the Grant-gated prod deploy (onboarding rebuild + live nginx /api/v1/ops/ route + WINDY_OPS_FLEET env pinned to the live service set). Until that deploy lands, treat these three tools as staged — verify with a live probe before relying on them mid-incident.

get_health.note: The fleet-health aggregator (windy-chat #143): overall ok/degraded/down (Synapse down ⇒ down) + per-service status. Prod deploy Grant-gated.

get_status.note: Same aggregator — every service's version + commit sha (did the deploy reach all of them?). Prod deploy Grant-gated.

get_capabilities.note: Same aggregator — which services are actually up = which capabilities work right now. Prod deploy Grant-gated.

get_logs.note: content-free only; NEVER expose message bodies (privacy hard line).

run_selftest.note: canary: send a message through Synapse + confirm a bridge delivers it.

reconnect.note: per-service; the aggregator's read view now shows WHICH service needs it — namespaced reconnect.<service> knobs are the natural next mutation pass.

restart_app.note: out-of-band (docker compose per service); ops plane outside the patient.

check_for_update.note: wire to the Steamroller fleet version manifest (admin /v1/fleet-versions is live).

reset_to_defaults.note: NEVER — Synapse holds real rooms + message history.

$headline_gap: CLOSED 2026-07-13 (windy-chat #143): the fleet-health aggregator exists — GET /api/v1/ops/health, EPT/JWT-gated, whitelist-scrubbed, per-service fan-out. Remaining headline work: the Grant-gated prod deploy that makes it reachable, then the mutation knobs the read view unlocks (per-service reconnect/restart, get_logs, run_selftest, SSH-free apply_update).

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST call when anything seems wrong with Windy Chat. One read returns the health of the WHOLE constellation: the Synapse homeserver that carries every message (if it's down, chat is down) plus every surrounding service — onboarding, directory, push, backup, social, translation, agent-roster, media, call-history, hub. Overall status is ok, degraded (some piece is down), or down (the homeserver heart itself is unreachable), with a per-service breakdown so you can see exactly WHICH piece failed instead of guessing.

status.description: ok | degraded | down for the whole constellation

services.description: per-service {status, version, commit, uptime, dependencies, duration_ms, error?}

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Read Windy Chat's deployment identity across the whole fleet in one call: every service's package version and git commit sha. Use this to answer 'did the fix that was supposed to deploy actually reach ALL the services, or is one still running the old build?' — the classic multi-service deploy question.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Probe which Windy Chat capabilities are actually working right now, rather than assuming. Each service that reports 'up' means its feature area works: media = photo/file sharing, push-gateway = notifications, hub = the Telegram/WhatsApp/Slack/Discord bridges, call-history = call logs, directory = finding contacts, agent-roster = your agents answering in chat, backup = encrypted backups, social = the social feed, translation = message translation. A service that is down means that capability is unavailable — everything else keeps working.

services.description: per-service {status} — up means the capability is available
